title: Indirect talks between the US and Iran end without progress and tensions persist in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Both sides maintain differences over the implementation of the nuclear memorandum and a possible transit fee, while Oman emerges as mediator.

title: Reform of the Imperial Household Law is prioritized on the parliamentary agenda and sparks debate over adoption of males
summary: The ruling party promotes discussion of the bill that would allow succession through adoption, while other legislative initiatives are postponed.

title: Japan and India agree on investments of 2 trillion yen in semiconductors, batteries, and artificial intelligence
summary: During the bilateral summit, more than 120 business cooperation agreements were signed to strengthen supply chains and technological innovation.

title: Heavy rains in Kyushu and the formation of two typhoons put Japan on alert
summary: Authorities warn of floods and landslides while monitoring the trajectory of the storms.

title: Tokyo's Sanyukai Hospital reports 20 cases in its 'baby hatch' and 7 confidential births in its first year of operation
summary: The program aims to protect mothers and newborns in vulnerable situations, offering safe alternatives to abandonment.

title: Naomi Osaka advances to second round at Wimbledon with a victory and a stunning white kimono
summary: The Japanese tennis player impressed both with her game and her traditional attire at the London grass tournament.

title: 20-year-old Kotaro Fukuoka becomes the new Honinbo go champion
summary: The young prodigy took the title from Ichiriki and achieved his first major championship in the professional go world.

title: Japan Football Association requests continuity of head coach Moriyasu and evaluates a friendly against Brazil
summary: The coach would be renewed for one year, while the inclusion of veteran players in the national team is debated.

title: United States wins with ten players and advances to the round of 16 of the World Cup
summary: Despite the expulsion of one of its players, the US team achieved a victory that qualifies it for the next round.

title: England comes from behind against the Democratic Republic of the Congo and qualifies for the round of 16
summary: The English team continues its path in the World Cup with a victory that brings it closer to its goal of winning the title after 60 years.

title: Mexico qualifies for the World Cup round of 16 but four fans die in celebrations
summary: The celebration for the Mexican team's advancement turned tragic with fatal casualties in the capital.

title: Japanese Foreign Minister Motegi promises support to Ukraine for the restoration of its cultural heritage
summary: During his visit to Kyiv, Motegi reaffirmed Japan's commitment to the reconstruction of Ukraine and the preservation of historical sites.

title: Nikkei index falls more than 1,500 points dragged down by decline in US tech stocks
summary: The Tokyo Stock Exchange closes below 69,000 yen amid fears of a correction in the artificial intelligence sector.

title: Yen weakens to 162.84 yen per dollar on expectations of rate hikes in the United States
summary: The Japanese currency continues its downward trend, pressured by the divergence of monetary policies and the strength of the dollar.

title: Japanese 10-year bond yield reaches 2.7%, its highest level in 29 years
summary: The rise in benchmark interest rates raises concerns about the cost of debt and the country's fiscal health.

title: France defeats Sweden and dedicates victory to its coach; Ecuador faces expulsion for anti-racism gesture
summary: The World Cup day also saw a sanction for an Ecuadorian player for covering his mouth and an artistic goal from Norwegian Nusa.

title: Trump praises Roosevelt, Supreme Court allows restricting trans athletes, and US rejects free trade
summary: The US administration advances a conservative agenda that includes protectionist trade policies and limits on trans rights.

title: Victor Willis, singer of 'Y.M.C.A.' and member of the Village People, dies at 74
summary: The American artist, known for disco culture anthems, passed away leaving a musical legacy.

title: Mount Fuji climbing season begins with entrance fees to control mass tourism
summary: Authorities implement a payment system to manage visitor influx at the iconic Japanese mountain.

title: Japan faces price increases on 20,000 food products due to inflation and the Middle East conflict
summary: The price rise aggravates pressure on Japanese households amid a weak yen and high energy costs.
